title: calophyllum aureum
intro: Calophyllum aureum, also known as the Kanuka tree or Kamani, is a species of evergreen tree native to tropical Asia and the Pacific Islands. The bark and seeds of Calophyllum aureum have been used in traditional medicine for their antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which are valued for their potential to treat a variety of health issues. The medicinal properties of Calophyllum aureum have been linked to its ability to promote wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate pain, as well as its potential to support cardiovascular health and immune function. Calophyllum aureum is commonly prepared as a herbal tincture, oil, or salve, which can be applied topically or taken internally under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
